name: MoneyGram
description_short: At 1610 E Camelback Rd, Phoenix, AZ 85016, MoneyGram can be reached at +1 800-746-7287.
description_long: MoneyGram is an established money transfer company that operates through a network of third-party agent locations rather than proprietary branches. The company enables customers to send and receive money domestically and internationally by partnering with various types of businesses including check cashing outlets, convenience stores, grocery retailers, and specialized money transfer shops. This agent-based model allows MoneyGram to maintain a wide geographic footprint without operating its own physical locations. In Phoenix alone, the company lists 48+ agent locations where customers can initiate transfers and pick up received funds. MoneyGram distinguishes itself through its hybrid service model—customers can manage transfers both online and offline, providing flexibility for those who prefer in-person transactions or lack reliable internet access. The company's agent network includes established check cashing chains like Ace Cash Express and Ez Check Cashing, as well as independent businesses like Yerberia Y Envios Tamayo and specialty retailers like CVS pharmacies. What sets MoneyGram apart is the accessibility of its service locations across diverse neighborhoods and business types, making money transfer available in areas that might otherwise lack such services. However, customers should understand that MoneyGram itself does not operate retail locations—they are fundamentally a payment processor working through partner agents. This means service quality, hours of operation, and fees may vary significantly by location. The agent-based model can also create confusion about which services each specific location offers, as not all agents provide all MoneyGram services. Additionally, the company's reliance on third-party operators means customer service and complaint resolution may be complicated by the need to work through both MoneyGram and the individual agent.
pros: Wide network of 48+ accessible agent locations across Phoenix in diverse neighborhoods
Partner locations include both major chains (CVS, Ace Cash Express) and independent local businesses
Hybrid service model allowing both online and offline transaction management
Multiple transaction types available including money sends and receives at single locations
Extended hours available through some agent locations (example: Elote Blanco open until 5:00 PM)
Serves underbanked communities through check cashing and specialty money services partners
No requirement for banking relationship to send or receive money
cons: MoneyGram does not operate proprietary locations—service quality and fees vary by third-party agent
Agent locations have inconsistent hours (ranging from 8:00 AM to 9:00 AM openings), limiting accessibility
Limited transparency on website regarding specific fees charged at different agent locations
Customer service complications due to agent-based model requiring coordination between MoneyGram and individual partners
Lack of guarantee that all 48+ locations offer identical service options or pricing
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers in Phoenix who need reliable money transfer access
Individuals sending money to family members internationally or domestically
Customers preferring in-person transactions over purely digital money transfer services
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked or underbanked Phoenix residents who need accessible, in-person money transfer services without a bank account. The primary caveat is that MoneyGram operates entirely through third-party agents rather than owned locations, meaning service quality, fees, hours, and available services vary significantly by specific location—customers must verify offerings at their chosen agent rather than assuming all locations are identical.
services: International money transfers (implied through agent network model)
Wire transfer processing (at select partner locations)
